title: hieracium piliferum var. subnivale
intro: Hieracium piliferum var. subnivale, also known as the Alpine Hawkweed, is a rare and little-studied species of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Hieracium piliferum var. subnivale include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
